page: Amazon Web Services r8gb.2xlarge is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the ARM64-based AWS Graviton processor family. It features 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs utilizing the ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 architecture running at 2.8 GHz. The processor configuration includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 36 MB of L3 cache. This instance is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local NVMe storage or GPU accelerators, and operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 17 Gbps.

Benchmark data highlights strong memory subsystem performance, with the Passmark memory score ranking in the top tier (top 10%). Single-threaded operations also perform well, as evidenced by top-25% results in Geekbench single-core testing, single-threaded gzip compression, and single-threaded bzip3 decompression. However, cryptographic and certain machine learning workloads show limitations; the Passmark CPU encryption test registers in the bottom 25%, and LLM prompt processing for medium-sized models falls into the bottom 10%. General multi-core compute, Redis operations, and static web serving throughput remain in the average tier.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r8gb.2xlarge offers a high-density memory profile on energy-efficient ARM architecture, making it a viable alternative to traditional x86 memory-optimized instances for workloads that do not require specialized AVX instruction sets. The absence of bundled local storage and GPUs positions this instance specifically for memory-bound, non-accelerated workloads. It is highly suited for in-memory databases, caching layers such as Redis, data buffering, and memory-intensive enterprise applications that benefit from high memory bandwidth and dedicated physical cores.
description: Amazon Web Services r8gb.2xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by an 8-core ARM64 AWS Graviton processor based on the ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 architecture. Operating at 2.8 GHz on the Nitro hypervisor, it features 8 dedicated vCPUs and 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high ratio of 8.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance lacks local storage and GPU hardware but provides a baseline network speed of 17 Gbps. Performance benchmarks indicate top-tier memory performance, with Passmark memory scores in the top 10%, alongside strong single-core and text-processing capabilities. Conversely, cryptographic and medium-model LLM prompt processing benchmarks show weaker relative performance. This instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-intensive workloads, making it well-suited for in-memory databases, caching layers, and memory-bound enterprise applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8gb.2xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized instance featuring 8 dedicated Graviton cores, 64 GB of RAM, and 17 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier memory performance for database workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r8gb.2xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized instance with 8 Graviton cores, 64 GB RAM, and top-tier memory benchmark performance.
tagline: An ARM64 memory-optimized virtual server featuring dedicated physical cores and high-bandwidth memory for memory-intensive database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an 8-core ARM64 AWS Graviton processor running at 2.8 GHz
Equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory (8.0 GB per core)
Delivers top-tier memory bandwidth and strong single-core performance
Features a 17 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Lacks local storage and GPU accelerators
Best suited for in-memory databases, caching layers, and memory-bound applications
